Title: Metabolic diversification of nitrogen-containing metabolites by the expression of a heterologous lysine decarboxylase gene in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes N-acetylcadaverine accumulation from ectopically produced cadaverine in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite profiling; prior literature cited for cadaverine activity; expression analysis in DC29
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Moderately increased in DC29 versus Col-0
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to detoxification/sequestration of excess diamines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNATA1 was proposed as the Arabidopsis enzyme catalyzing N-acetylation of cadaverine to N-acetylcadaverine. 2. Its expression was moderately increased in the high-La-L/ODC line DC29. 3. The study supports co-option of endogenous promiscuous enzymes for diversification of cadaverine-derived metabolites.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be co-engineered to channel cadaverine into less toxic conjugated forms during lysine-derived alkaloid pathway engineering.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive heterologous La-L/ODC expression in seedlings
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert flux away from other desired cadaverine-derived products; exact in planta contribution not directly proven.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly biochemically assayed in this paper.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes p-coumaroylcadaverine formation from cadaverine and p-coumaroyl-CoA in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite profiling; prior literature cited for putrescine conjugation activity; expression analysis in DC lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely detoxification/storage of excess polyamines and contribution to chemodiversity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtACT expression increased in DC lines with elevated cadaverine/putrescine. 2. p-Coumaroylcadaverine was detected specifically in transgenic lines. 3. AtACT was proposed to catalyze cadaverine conjugation analogously to its known action on putrescine.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering phenolamide sinks to buffer polyamine toxicity during cadaverine pathway introduction.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could consume phenylpropanoid acyl donors and affect phenylpropanoid allocation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Feruloylcadaverine was not detected, likely due to substrate preference and/or low abundance.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes de novo cadaverine accumulation and initiates broad diversification into cadaverine-derived metabolites in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase; ornithine/lysine decarboxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lysine-derived cadaverine biosynthesis / entry step to cadaverine catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Branch point from primary lysine metabolism to cadaverine and Lys-derived alkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed branch point / upstream entry step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates L-lysine to cadaverine; also can decarboxylate L-ornithine to putrescine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and supplies precursor pool for downstream cadaverine-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR; targeted LC-MS quantification; untargeted metabolomics; 15N-lysine feeding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in DC29, moderate in DC21 and DC42 under transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Double CaMV 35S promoter with omega enhancer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cadaverine acts as precursor for Lys-derived alkaloids and expands plant chemodiversity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Heterologous La-L/ODC expression caused cadaverine accumulation in Arabidopsis, absent from wild type. 2. Cadaverine levels correlated with transgene expression level across independent lines. 3. La-L/ODC expression reduced lysine and increased putrescine, and triggered production of multiple cadaverine-derived metabolites including 5-aminopentanal, 5-aminopentanoate and d-valerolactam. 4. 15N-labeled lysine feeding confirmed that the new metabolites were synthesized from lysine via symmetric cadaverine.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High-expression line DC29 showed reduced root growth and biomass; lysine pool decreased; major increase in putrescine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central experimental gene of the paper; transgene from a quinolizidine alkaloid-producing species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A powerful entry gene for engineering cadaverine and lysine-derived alkaloid precursor production, but should be balanced with downstream detoxification/catabolic modules to avoid growth penalties and lysine depletion.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely oxidizes cadaverine to 5-aminopentanal, enabling downstream production of 5-aminopentanoate, d-valerolactam and alkaloid-like metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to 5-aminopentanoate, d-valerolactam, and piperideine-derived alkaloid-like metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; proposed key entry enzyme of oxidative catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite identification in transgenic lines; prior literature cited for cadaverine substrate activity; phylogenetic analysis; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Marginally upregulated in DC29 versus Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 5-Aminopentanal accumulated specifically in La-L/ODC lines. 2. AtCuAO3 is a strong candidate for its formation because prior work showed cadaverine oxidation activity and phylogenetic clustering with known cadaverine-active CuAOs. 3. AtCuAO3 expression was marginally increased in DC29.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May also act on other aliphatic amines and influence polyamine homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed rather than directly assayed in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate co-expression partner with L/ODC to channel cadaverine into downstream specialized metabolites rather than free diamine accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts 5-aminopentanal to 5-aminopentanoate, supporting downstream cadaverine catabolism in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate product in cadaverine catabolic route leading to d-valerolactam
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay in E. coli; LC-MS product detection; expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Slightly increased in DC29 compared with Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant AtALDH10A8 converted 5-aminopentanal to 5-aminopentanoate in vitro. 2. This supports co-option of Arabidopsis ALDH enzymes for catabolism of newly formed cadaverine intermediates. 3. Expression was slightly increased in DC29.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay with recombinant protein; La-L/ODC-expressing seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could also affect native aminoaldehyde/GABA-related metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known for 4-aminobutanal to GABA conversion; new substrate promiscuity shown here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream module to convert reactive aminoaldehydes into more stable products during cadaverine engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts 5-aminopentanal to 5-aminopentanoate, supporting downstream cadaverine catabolism in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate product in cadaverine catabolic route leading to d-valerolactam
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay in E. coli; LC-MS product detection; expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Slightly increased in DC29 compared with Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant AtALDH10A9 converted 5-aminopentanal to 5-aminopentanoate in vitro. 2. It functions similarly to AtALDH10A8 as a co-opted enzyme for cadaverine intermediate catabolism. 3. Expression was slightly increased in DC29.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay with recombinant protein; La-L/ODC-expressing seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could perturb native GABA/aminoaldehyde metabolism if engineered strongly.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known for 4-aminobutanal oxidation; cadaverine-pathway role demonstrated here through substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream enzyme to stabilize flux from cadaverine oxidation in synthetic pathways.
